사도행전 21:1.content: When we had departed from them and had set sail, we came with a straight course to Cos, and the next day to Rhodes, and from there to Patara.

사도행전 21:2.content: Having found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, we went aboard, and set sail.

사도행전 21:3.content: When we had come in sight of Cyprus, leaving it on the left hand, we sailed to Syria and landed at Tyre, for the ship was there to unload her cargo.

사도행전 21:4.content: Having found disciples, we stayed there seven days. These said to Paul through the Spirit that he should not go up to Jerusalem.

사도행전 21:5.content: When those days were over, we departed and went on our journey. They all, with wives and children, brought us on our way until we were out of the city. Kneeling down on the beach, we prayed.

사도행전 21:6.content: After saying goodbye to each other, we went on board the ship, and they returned home again.

사도행전 21:7.content: When we had finished the voyage from Tyre, we arrived at Ptolemais. We greeted the brothers and stayed with them one day.

사도행전 21:8.content: On the next day, we who were Paul’s companions departed, and came to Caesarea. We entered into the house of Philip the evangelist, who was one of the seven, and stayed with him.

사도행전 21:9.content: Now this man had four virgin daughters who prophesied.

사도행전 21:10.content: As we stayed there some days, a certain prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.

사도행전 21:11.content: Coming to us and taking Paul’s belt, he bound his own feet and hands, and said, “The Holy Spirit says: ‘So the Jews at Jerusalem will bind the man who owns this belt, and will deliver him into the hands of the Gentiles.’ ”

사도행전 21:12.content: When we heard these things, both we and the people of that place begged him not to go up to Jerusalem.

사도행전 21:13.content: Then Paul answered, “What are you doing, weeping and breaking my heart? For I am ready not only to be bound, but also to die at Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.”

사도행전 21:14.content: When he would not be persuaded, we ceased, saying, “The Lord’s will be done.”

사도행전 21:15.content: After these days we took up our baggage and went up to Jerusalem.

사도행전 21:16.content: Some of the disciples from Caesarea also went with us, bringing one Mnason of Cyprus, an early disciple, with whom we would stay.

사도행전 21:17.content: When we had come to Jerusalem, the brothers received us gladly.

사도행전 21:18.content: The day following, Paul went in with us to James; and all the elders were present.

사도행전 21:19.content: When he had greeted them, he reported one by one the things which God had worked among the Gentiles through his ministry.

사도행전 21:20.content: They, when they heard it, glorified God. They said to him, “You see, brother, how many thousands there are among the Jews of those who have believed, and they are all zealous for the law.

사도행전 21:21.content: They have been informed about you, that you teach all the Jews who are among the Gentiles to forsake Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children and not to walk after the customs.

사도행전 21:22.content: What then? The assembly must certainly meet, for they will hear that you have come.

사도행전 21:23.content: Therefore do what we tell you. We have four men who have taken a vow.

사도행전 21:24.content: Take them and purify yourself with them, and pay their expenses for them, that they may shave their heads. Then all will know that there is no truth in the things that they have been informed about you, but that you yourself also walk keeping the law.

사도행전 21:25.content: But concerning the Gentiles who believe, we have written our decision that they should observe no such thing, except that they should keep themselves from food offered to idols, from blood, from strangled things, and from sexual immorality.”

사도행전 21:26.content: Then Paul took the men, and the next day purified himself and went with them into the temple, declaring the fulfillment of the days of purification, until the offering was offered for every one of them.

사도행전 21:27.content: When the seven days were almost completed, the Jews from Asia, when they saw him in the temple, stirred up all the multitude and laid hands on him,

사도행전 21:28.content: crying out, “Men of Israel, help! This is the man who teaches all men everywhere against the people, and the law, and this place. Moreover, he also brought Greeks into the temple, and has defiled this holy place!”

사도행전 21:29.content: For they had seen Trophimus, the Ephesian, with him in the city, and they supposed that Paul had brought him into the temple.

사도행전 21:30.content: All the city was moved and the people ran together. They seized Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the doors were shut.

사도행전 21:31.content: As they were trying to kill him, news came up to the commanding officer of the regiment that all Jerusalem was in an uproar.

사도행전 21:32.content: Immediately he took soldiers and centurions and ran down to them. They, when they saw the chief captain and the soldiers, stopped beating Paul.

사도행전 21:33.content: Then the commanding officer came near, arrested him, commanded him to be bound with two chains, and inquired who he was and what he had done.

사도행전 21:34.content: Some shouted one thing, and some another, among the crowd. When he couldn’t find out the truth because of the noise, he commanded him to be brought into the barracks.

사도행전 21:35.content: When he came to the stairs, he was carried by the soldiers because of the violence of the crowd;

사도행전 21:36.content: for the multitude of the people followed after, crying out, “Away with him!”

사도행전 21:37.content: As Paul was about to be brought into the barracks, he asked the commanding officer, “May I speak to you?” He said, “Do you know Greek?

사도행전 21:38.content: Aren’t you then the Egyptian, who before these days stirred up to sedition and led out into the wilderness the four thousand men of the Assassins?”

사도행전 21:39.content: But Paul said, “I am a Jew, from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of no insignificant city. I beg you, allow me to speak to the people.”

사도행전 21:40.content: When he had given him permission, Paul, standing on the stairs, beckoned with his hand to the people. When there was a great silence, he spoke to them in the Hebrew language, saying,
